currentFocus: Close release-blocking filesystem authority gaps: save/options guarded output is implemented and green; trusted asset-root discovery and package materialization remain active before WinUI UX and rebuild work.

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

gitBaseline.ordinaryCampaignBranchCleanup: complete: 61 obsolete local campaign/codex branch names removed after baseline acceptance

repositoryScale.contributorQuickCheck: npm test -> check:quick passed in 91.4 seconds (docsync, doc commands, zero-warning WinUI build, 1217 AppCore tests, 134 WinUI tests with 2 expected real-asset skips)

safetyAudit.safeCopyModel: Retain it. Initial copied-profile creation, copied-executable target checks, catalog pinning, backup provenance, and process stop targeting are strong.
safetyAudit.patchCatalog: 29 total rows; 20 visible (9 stable, 11 experimental); 9 hidden companions.
closedThisWave: Save/options outputs now require matching .bes/.bea types, calculate the app-owned patched-output default without creating it, resolve DOS drive aliases to physical local paths, hold physical ancestor identities, protect source file identities, reject in-place/hardlink/symlink/junction/device/network/alternate-stream/reserved-device and installed-game-tree targets, stage and flush a sibling file, atomically replace the destination, and verify staged plus committed identities and bytes.

Generated safe-copy control editing remains available only after profile/manifest validation mints a capability for the canonical app-owned GameProfiles root; a naked trusted-root string can no longer authorize writes.
safetyAudit.acceptance: Final bounded normal and adversarial reviewers both returned ACCEPT after earlier REJECT findings drove trusted-root capability, physical path, staging-handle, no-follow final-handle, and deterministic race-test corrections.
highPriorityGaps: Asset catalogs can resolve rooted, ancestor, or current-directory sources instead of one declared trusted export root.
mediumPriorityGaps: Launch attestation does not fully revalidate mutable profile options/save/data identities after profile creation.
Control/music content can commit before its accounting manifest.
Copied executable writes are verified but not committed through a fully atomic replacement transaction.

uxAudit.runtimeScope: Two isolated launches, including a no-game first run, covered Home, Save Lab, Game Options, Windowed & Mods, Media, Asset Library, Lore, Settings, and About.
uxAudit.verifiedBuildAndTests: WinUI build passed with zero warnings/errors; UI tests passed 134 with 2 asset-dependent skips; AppCore passed 1240 after save/options safety hardening, including live SUBST physical-alias and deterministic staging/final-entry race coverage.
priorityFindings: Show debug trace can leave a launch preset falsely selected even though launch arguments change.
Cached navigation can retain stale live-region status and off-screen focus.
The safe-copy BEA.exe source action clips at the tested compact width.
True first run promotes Save Lab over the missing game-folder setup action.
Six principal pages lack semantic Level1 headings and consistent arrival focus.

boundaries: Installed game and original BEA.exe remain read-only.
No release, signing, installer/MSIX/Store, live Ghidra mutation, gameplay, online-ready, rebuild-parity, or visual-parity claim is created by the baseline slice.
Static RE accounting and source-derived names remain evidence inputs, not runtime behavior proof.

activeBlockers: Cursor/Grok read-only consult is degraded: Windows lacks the required sandbox and WSL lacks separate model-catalog authentication.
Asset catalog and package-materialization filesystem identity boundaries need hardening before stronger safety claims or feature expansion.
nextSteps: Bind asset catalogs, sidecar discovery, package sources, and package destinations to explicit trusted roots with hostile-path tests.
Fix launch-preset equality and navigation status/focus, then address compact layout, setup hierarchy, and page headings.
Define the rebuild evidence firewall and build the deterministic kernel.
